human_actions_pending.context: Tests product-market signal before more engineering. Anjay's reaction shapes Phase 2 vs Phase 3 priority

human_actions_pending.action: Kill the localhost:3082 dev server (background process b2jqbb3dj) if pausing for more than ~24h
human_actions_pending.context: Server is still running. Harmless but eats RAM

decisions.decision: Use Roy's personal Supabase project (lkkljxvuhkaydhffpaix, eu-central-1 Frankfurt) for dev; client account when prod ships
decisions.rationale: Transfer is trivial (re-run migrations); Anjay's account wasn't ready; speed > clean ownership at the dev stage

decisions.decision: Session Pooler (port 5432, IPv4-proxied) for both DATABASE_URL and DIRECT_URL
decisions.rationale: Direct connection is IPv6-only on free tier; most Kenyan ISPs are IPv4. Session pooler works for both runtime AND prisma migrate, eliminating the need for two pooler URLs at the dev stage

decisions.decision: Dev server port 3082 instead of default 3000
decisions.rationale: Roy's :3000 was busy. Baked into package.json's `dev` script

decisions.decision: Skip §A SQLite baseline boot in RUNBOOK
decisions.rationale: Codex flagged §A as belt-and-suspenders; §D Postgres boot covers the same FND-01 ground without the rollback/replay dance

decisions.decision: Apply Codex's 6 review findings inline via Edit tool rather than re-spawning planner with --reviews
decisions.rationale: Faster, Roy stays in the loop, surgical patches preserve plan structure

decisions.decision: Plan 02 added forecastRunId @default(cuid()) at schema level (codex catch #4)
decisions.rationale: Migration-safe — if a future prod DB has existing predictions, the default backfills cleanly. The route still supplies a single batch ID per run; the default only fires for orphan writes

background_processes.purpose: Dev server for Playwright verification during Wave 2/3
background_processes.kill_when_resuming: optional — Next.js Turbopack will hot-reload Phase 2/3 edits if you leave it up

next_action: /gsd:resume-work to load this handoff, then either /gsd:discuss-phase 2 --auto (canonical roadmap order) OR /gsd:discuss-phase 3 --auto (single-tenant Shopify-first shortcut). Recommend asking Anjay first.
context_notes: Phase 1 was the boring foundation — Postgres + determinism + onOrder feedback + dedupe. Live-verified end-to-end via Playwright: dashboard renders Beauty Square's 1023-product catalog at localhost:3082 with KES 11.6M synthetic-sales revenue, 109 critical/high reorders, 130 stockouts. Two consecutive forecast runs produce byte-identical math (FND-02 mulberry32 works). Approving 'Glow From Within Bundle' bumped Product.onOrder 0→65 transactionally; next forecast recommended 0 instead of 65 (FND-04 + codex #1 closed). All work is in git on `main` of the local clone at `wezesha/stock-estimator/`, not yet pushed upstream. The next decision is strategic (demo Anjay first OR ship Phase 2 auth foundation) rather than technical — that's why this is paused-pending-decision rather than paused-mid-task. Roy's session preferences: grade-9 explanations, concrete options + recommendations in AskUserQuestion, CLI-first execution, ask before destructive operations. Codex CLI is installed and proven valuable as a second-opinion reviewer (caught 6 real issues plan-checker missed). Supabase agent-skills available via `npx skills add supabase/agent-skills` (deferred — not installed).
